dialogos.texto_en: I feel like we just passed through this street earlier.
Oh dear, we're in trouble.\nCould it be that we're...lost?
What are the two of you wandering around for?\nLost your way?
Oh my, isn't that Uesugi-kun?\nWhat a coincidence to meet you in a place like this.
By the way, we're not lost or anything!\nWe're just looking for a shop that was \nrecommended to us!
Oh, are you planning to go somewhere?
Yes, there should be a delicious pancake cafe nearby.
A clerk at a shop we stopped by \non the way here suggested it\nbut this area is so complicated...
(...Speaking of sweets, could this be Nino?)
Yeah, that's right.\nThere are many side streets here, like a maze.\nBut you can make pancakes at home, can't you?
That's...
That's true.\nIf I asked Nino or Miku,\nthey could probably make them at home.
But, Uesugi-kun, you don't understand, at all!
Wh-what?
Just as handmade products have their own charm,\nevery restaurant has its own unique taste.
Besides, the ingredients and equipment you \nwould use differ between at home and at a shop.
I see, that makes sense.
Plus, it's recommended by the locals.\nWe absolutely have to try it!
So, anyway.\nCould you please help us find the shop, Uesugi-kun?\nWe'll treat you as a thank you.
Well, guess that's fine with me.
(Shortly afterwards, we were able to find\n the pancake shop, but it was even more stylish\n than I had expected.)
(Of course, the inside of the shop was filled\n with only female customers.\n I felt out of place and held back, but%p-2;――%p;)
Th-these are the delicious pancakes that \neveryone's been talking about...!\nJust looking at them makes me feel happy.
I also can't get enough of this sweet aroma!\nYou can feel its deliciousness\neven before eating it.
I said it's fine...
But, Uesugi-kun?\nYou're not even saying anything about it.\nThat's quite impolite, you know?
No, that's not it.\nLook around, there are only female customers.
I'm the only male, aren't I?\nI'm completely out of place...
You don't need to worry about that.
Well, I do.
The other customers are also captivated\nby their pancakes in front of them.\nNobody gives a hoot about you.
Ah, well now that you mention it, that's true.
I've thought this before,\nbut aren't you a bit self-conscious?
That's right. It's too much to think\nthat you'll get attention for something like this.
Uuh...
And, men tend to be shy in situations like this%p-2;――
Delicious food is delicious,\nso just say that you like it with confidence!
You easily say that, but that's difficult.\nIsn't it strange to not care about the opinions\nof those around you in this kinda situation?
*sigh*\nYou really are self-conscious, aren't you?
Well, since the pancakes are already in front of you,\nyou should prepare yourself to eat them.
You'll probably stop caring about what others think too.
Now then!\nIt's disrespectful to just keep staring at these pancakes.
I don't really like pancakes, though.
You're not being honest, are you?\nI'm talking about preferences in general,\nnot just yours.
Well, yeah, it's kinda weird\nto pretend to hate something you actually like.
That's right.\nSuppressing your desires\ncan also be harmful to your health.
And you're not alone here,\nso let's enjoy ourselves openly.
Now then!\nIt's disrespectful to just keep staring at these pancakes.
Maybe you're right.\nIt's a simple thing,\nbut I tend to overthink things and make it more difficult.
Well, that's a bad habit of yours, isn't it?
And you often end up failing because of it, don't you?\nSometimes it's good to act without thinking too much,\nand just be straightforward.
Now then!\nIt's disrespectful to just keep staring at these pancakes.
That's right.\nWell then, let's savor it with care.
O-okay.\nThat sounds a little pompous, but I'll give it a shot.
Time to eat!
Wow, I'm shocked.\nWhat's with this fluffiness?\nIs this actual food?
It is food, but I agree it's quite remarkable.
This is... Teehee!\nIt's so delicious that I can't help but laugh.
It feels like I'm savoring happiness...\nEvery bite makes me gasp...
(Haha...\n There are two Itsukis in front of me...)
Haaa...\nMy stomach and heart are both thoroughly satisfied.
If I could eat this every day,\nhow happy I would be...
It was truly delicious.\nWe should thank the clerk who recommended it.
(They both look so happy.\n I can see both of them as Itsuki.)
Did you perhaps think that there were two Itsukis here?
Oof?!
I am Itsuki, you know?\nDon't mistake me for someone else.
No, I am the real Itsuki.
...I wonder about that.\nIf you were the real Itsuki,\nwouldn't you be eating more?
Oh, well, if you're the one treating,\nI'll eat as much as you want.
Well then, let's do that.\nLet's go back to the restaurant!
No, that's... I'm sorry, spare me!
